comment.body:
no.
short RR trade is a synthetic short stock trade. Long put, short call.
that was the pnl for today. the aggregated pnl.
don't give too much significance to it, bcs positioning is an aggregation of millions of different positions with different intentions and strats.

MM speed was net negative, with heavy upside vol bid (contradicting to the short rr trade, but i said above, why)
i gave the levels, you have edu posts about that.. levels help you to understand the momentum

comment.body:
3) these sentiment projections are not holy scriptums.
they can be wrong sometimes. 
again: if i have 60% accuracy with these, then there will be 4.8 month, 100.8 days in a year when my posts or projections will be inaccurate at some part...

this is not a discord copy-trading service. 
I provide analyses, daily and weekly + macro/geo context (since Trump, these two are tied heavily together)
so ,don't be married that "this day is bearish, or bullish" not to mention, that i'm not a zookeeper, and i rarely use this dualistic wording. 
you get the levels and the forecasted dynamics.
there can be divergences and convergences.
both event is very constructive

comment.body:
just to give you a very simple example:
if there is a dealer short RR position (negative speed), with negative zomma, and logn vomma, (lets say)
market is supported to the downside, bcs downside vol is sold, right? and as vol goes down, spot goes down, gamma goes up = downside becomes even more supported, even tho vol has a limit due to vomma supply.

you get a pivot level, if it is confirmed to be broken, and you see the dynamics playing out, you can be sure that momentum will turn on, and some downside clusters become gravitational.. or at least, thats what the customers expect.

also, to the upside, above a certain pivot, momentum catches a bid, vol goes up, gamma goes down, move accelerates towards a certain cluster, what the customers expect, right?
again: tradeable (but again, whats your strat: vol trading? pair trading? hedging? momentum trading? which momentum? etc.)

similarly to the upside..
